title: Little endian indexing of packed multidimensional arrays is incorrectly assumed to be big endian
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.22+1 (git sha1 c4a52b1b028, gcc 12.2.1 -O2 -fexceptions -fstack-protector-strong -m64 -mtune=generic -fasynchronous-unwind-tables -fstack-clash-protection -fcf-protection -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I have added a test to tests/svtypes/struct_array.sv to demonstrate that indexing misbehaves - see diff below:
[struct_array_le.diff.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/9983714/struct_array_le.diff.gz)

The asserts have been verified with Verilator.
Note that iverilog has an off-by-one error in little endian array slices, and can thus not be used for verification.

Replacing the `initial` block for assigments to `s3` with the output of sv2v makes the test pass:

```
	initial begin
		s3 = 1'sb0;
		s3[24+:16] = 16'h1234;
		s3[56+:8] = 8'h42;
		s3[72+:8] = 1'sb1;
		s3[73-:2] = 1'sb0;
		s3[15-:16] = 1'sb1;
		s3[1:0] = 1'sb0;
	end
```

### Expected Behavior

The augmented array test passes.

### Actual Behavior

The array test fails.
